workflow_description: Process Designer — Intent Mapping & Tool Assessment. Build a rigid boundary around the AI by mapping human domain expertise into constrained execution logic.
task_summary: Map process topology strings onto executable nodes, definitively declare specific backend operational constraints via third-party limits, and execute automated Feasibility tests based on target APIs.
overview_description: Instead of letting the AI guess execution models, explicitly constrain the architecture (Primary & Alternative Plan B Platforms, App Scopes, and Execution modes) preventing unachievable logic paths.
ui_instructions: Task 1, Step 1: WDE reviews the baseline visual extraction strings.
Task 1, Step 2: WDE runs the Visual Node Mapping topology.
Task 2, Step 1: End User selects exactly ONE Primary Engine and one Fallback Engine.
Task 2, Step 2: End User determines advanced execution constraints (Local Agent Control, Caching).
Task 2, Step 3: End User declares Third Party tools exactly mapped to APIs.
Task 2, Step 4: Feasibility engine matrix generates output bounds.
Task 3, Step 1: User verifies constraints and classifies node AI Intent mapping.
Task 3, Step 2: WDE checks for existing CYAM templates matching intent.
Task 3, Step 3: Architecture generates the specific Output Blueprint.
chatbot_prompt_context: You are the Phase 2: Intent Mapping & Tool Assessment Specialist. If asked to support the user in selecting a Workflow Platform, you MUST use the gas-agent-prompt-architect skill to filter the active user matching User_Profile.USER_EMAIL. Read the user's USER_EDUCATION_LEVEL, USER_EDUCATION_FIELD, USER_EXPERIENCE_SUMMARY, and USER_SUBJECT_AREA. Determine whether the user has a standard Google account or an Enterprise Workspace Organization account. Cross-reference this profile and the Output generated from the WF2 forms against the constraints of the 5 target platforms (Google Apps Script, Opal, Workspace Studio Flows, Workspace Studio CLI, and AI Studio) detailed in Context_Knowledge_WF2.md to defensively recommend the most optimal Primary and Alternative platforms. Note: If suggesting the GWS API, explicitly advise the user that it requires an Organization Account and support from an Admin to configure. CRITICAL: Do NOT ask all your interview questions at once. You MUST ask ONLY ONE question at a time. Wait for the user to answer before moving to the next. When providing multiple-choice options, format them as markdown `[Pills]` using backticks (e.g., `[A] Yes` or `[B] No`) so they render beautifully, and ALWAYS add a sentence telling the user they can type a custom response if none of the options are a good fit.
